author: sinorga
text: asizeof get wrong size with numpy arrays after slicing

author: sinorga
text:
Its similar with [#36](https://github.com/pympler/pympler/issues/36)

Environment:  
ubuntu 1804, 64 bit  
python 3.7.5  
numpy 1.17.3  
Pympler 0.8

---

```
>>> import numpy as np
>>> from pympler import asizeof
>>> arr = np.arange(1000)
>>> asizeof.asizeof(arr)
8096
>>> arr = arr[:]
>>> asizeof.asizeof(arr)
96
```

The size is expected to the same.

author: mrJean1
text:
Thank you. So far, I'm able to duplicate the issue with asizeof 19.03.29 using Python 2.7.18 and numpy 1.16.5, with Python 3.7.6 and numpy 1.17.2 and with Python 3.8.2 and numpy 1.18.0, all in 64-bit and on macOS 10.13.6.

The result may be unexpected, but is correct. Using the example below, both objects `a` and `b` are an instance of `numpy.ndarray`, but only object `a` includes the memory for the 1,000 items. Object `b` is a `slice` of `a` and refers to object `a` through the `.base` attribute of `numpy.ndarray` objects documented [here](https://numpy.org/devdocs/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.base.html).

```
>>> a = numpy.arange(1000)
>>> asizeof.asizeof(a), a.base
8096, None

>>> b = a[:]
>>> asizeof.asizeof(b), b.base is a
96, True
```

To illustrate the difference:

```
>>> b = numpy.array(a[:])
>>> asizeof.asizeof(b), b.base
8096, None
```

Perhaps, `asizeof` should be enhanced to handle the `.base` attribute `numpy` objects like *references*, similar to `list` and `tuple` items or `dict` keys and values ?

author: mrJean1
text: After the patch **Handle NumPy slices**, the `asizeof` slice `b` will include and be slightly larger than the size of `a`. Likewise, the combined `asizeof(a, b)` will just be the size of `b`.

author: Pankrat
text: Thanks [@sinorga](https://github.com/sinorga) and [@mrJean1](https://github.com/mrJean1)! Your fix has been included in v0.9.
